volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: The Little Carthaginian (Section 3.4.717b - Section 3.5.765)

sections.label: Section 3.4.717b
sections.translation: I follow you .

sections.label: Section 3.4.717c
sections.translation: Well , well , walk on ; and then we ’ ll talk together about the other matters that remain .

sections.label: Section 3.4.719
sections.translation: As for me , I ’ ll tell you about the Spartan affairs .

sections.label: Section 3.4.720
sections.translation: Why then follow me .

sections.label: Section 3.4.720b
sections.translation: Lead me in-doors ; lead me in , you have got me made over to you .

sections.label: Section 3.4.721
sections.translation: What do you advise me now ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.721b
sections.translation: To be moderate .

sections.label: Section 3.4.722
sections.original: Quid si animus esse non sinit ?
sections.translation: What if my feelings will not let me be ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.722b
sections.translation: Then be as they will let you .

sections.label: Section 3.4.723
sections.translation: Did you see it , when the Procurer received the money ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.723b
sections.translation: We saw it .

sections.label: Section 3.4.724
sections.original: Eum vos meum esse servom scitis ?
sections.translation: Did you know that he is my slave ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.724b
sections.translation: We knew it .

sections.label: Section 3.4.725
sections.translation: That it is a thing against the reiterated laws of the people ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.725b
sections.translation: We knew it .

sections.label: Section 3.4.726
sections.original: Em istaec volo ergo vos commeminisse omnia ,
sections.translation: Well then , all these things I wish you to keep in memory before the Praetor by-and-by , when occasion shall come .

sections.label: Section 3.4.727b
sections.translation: We remember them .

sections.label: Section 3.4.728
sections.original: Quid si recenti re aedis pultem ?
sections.translation: What if , while the matter has so recently happened , I knock at the door ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.728b
sections.translation: I think you ought .

sections.label: Section 3.4.729
sections.translation: If I do knock , he won ’ t open it [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.4.729b
sections.translation: Then break the pannel [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.4.730
sections.original: Si exierit leno , quid tum ? hominem interrogem ,
sections.translation: If the Procurer comes out , do you think I ought to enquire of the fellow whether my slave has come to him or not ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.731b
sections.translation: Why not ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.732
sections.translation: With two hundred golden Philippean pieces ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.732b
sections.translation: Why not ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.733
sections.translation: Then the Procurer will be going astray at once .

sections.label: Section 3.4.733b
sections.translation: About what matter ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.733c
sections.translation: Do you ask ? Because a less sum will be named by one hundred pieces .

sections.label: Section 3.4.734b
sections.translation: You judge rightly .

sections.label: Section 3.4.735
sections.translation: He ’ ll think that some other person is being looked after .

sections.label: Section 3.4.735b
sections.translation: No doubt .

sections.label: Section 3.4.736
sections.translation: He ’ ll be denying it at once .

sections.label: Section 3.4.736b
sections.translation: On his oath even .

sections.label: Section 3.4.737
sections.translation: The fellow will involve himself in the guilt of theft —

sections.label: Section 3.4.737b
sections.translation: Beyond a doubt , it certainly is so .

sections.label: Section 3.4.738
sections.original: Quantum quantum ad eum erit delatum .
sections.translation: Of however much it is that shall have been brough to him .

sections.label: Section 3.4.738b
sections.translation: Why not ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.739
sections.translation: Jupiter confound you [*] !

sections.label: Section 3.4.739b
sections.translation: Why not your own self ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.740
sections.translation: I ’ ll go and knock at this door .

sections.label: Section 3.4.740b
sections.translation: Even so . Why not ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.741
sections.original: Tacendi tempus est , nam crepuerunt fores .
sections.translation: It ’ s time to be quiet , for the door makes a noise . I see the Procurer Lycus coming out of doors ; come this way , pray !

sections.label: Section 3.4.743b
sections.translation: Why not ? But , if you please , cover up our heads , that the Procurer mayn ’ t know us ,

sections.label: Section 3.4.745
sections.original: qui illi malae rei tantae fuimus inlices .
sections.translation: who have been his decoyers [*] into so great a calamity .

sections.label: Section 3.5.746
sections.translation: Let all soothsayers go hang themselves now at once . Why should I believe them in future , as to what they say ? For they , just now at the sacrifice , told me that evil and the greatest disaster was portended to me .

sections.label: Section 3.5.750
sections.original: is explicavi meam rem postilla lucro .
sections.translation: I have since then amplified my fortune with profit .

sections.label: Section 3.5.751
sections.translation: Save you , Procurer .

sections.label: Section 3.5.751b
sections.translation: May the Gods bless you , Agorastocles .

sections.label: Section 3.5.752
sections.original: Magis mé benigne nunc salutas quam antidhac .
sections.translation: You now salute me more kindly than hitherto .

sections.label: Section 3.5.753
sections.original: Tranquillitas evenit , quasi navi in mari :
sections.translation: A calm has come , as though to a ship at sea . Just as the wind is , to that quarter is the sail shifted .

sections.label: Section 3.5.755
sections.original: Valeant apud te quos volo ; atque haud te volo .
sections.translation: May those ladies be well in your house , to whom I wish it , but to yourself I do not wish it .

sections.label: Section 3.5.756
sections.original: Valent ut postulatumst , verum non tibi .
sections.translation: They are well , as you desire ; not for you , though .

sections.label: Section 3.5.757
sections.original: Mitte ad me , si audes , hodie Adelphasium tuam ,
sections.translation: Send your Adelphasium to my house , to-day , please , upon this celebrated and famous festival , the Aphrodisia .

sections.label: Section 3.5.759
sections.original: Calidum prandisti prandium hodie ? dic mihi .
sections.translation: Have you been breakfasting on a hot breakfast today ? Tell me .

sections.label: Section 3.5.760
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.760b
sections.original: Quia os nunc frigefactas , quom rogas .
sections.translation: Because now you are only cooling your mouth [*] , when you ask me .

sections.label: Section 3.5.761
sections.original: Hoc age sis , leno . servom esse audivi meum
sections.translation: Attend to this , Procurer , will you ; I ’ ve heard that my slave is at your house .

sections.label: Section 3.5.762b
sections.translation: At my house ? You ’ ll find that has never been the fact .

sections.label: Section 3.5.763
sections.original: Mentire . nam ad te venit aurumque attulit .
sections.translation: You lie ; for he has come to your house , and carried off some gold there . Word has been brought me to that effect , by persons I fully believe .

sections.label: Section 3.5.765
sections.original: Malus es , captatum me advenis cum testibus .
sections.translation: You are an artful fellow : you ’ ve come to entrap me with your witnesses . There ’ s no one of your people in my house , nor anything of yours .
